BANNER_DATA.heading: Imagine hearing the way it should be. Know where to go.
BANNER_DATA.description: A nationwide network connecting patients with qualified hearing-care professionals within 25 miles—ensuring personalized, trusted care close to home.

PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.title: Precise Hearing Assessment & Testing
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.intro: Our evidence-based diagnostic evaluations identify the exact nature and degree of your hearing loss — giving you and your specialist a clear, actionable roadmap to better hearing.
list: Detailed hearing history and symptom review
Medical condition screening and risk identification
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.closing: These evaluations help ensure that hearing concerns are addressed correctly—before any treatment decisions are made.

stats.description: Americans with hearing loss go undiagnosed
Improvement in outcomes with early detection
Complete evaluation in a single session

signsSection.heading: Signs You May Need an Assessment
listItems: You frequently ask people to repeat themselves
You struggle to follow conversations in noisy places
You hear ringing or buzzing in your ears (tinnitus)
Family members notice you raise the TV volume
You have occupational noise exposure history
You're 50+ and haven't had a recent hearing check

cards.title: Targeted Diagnosis
cards.description: Pinpoint whether your hearing loss is sensorineural, conductive, or mixed for proper treatment planning.

cards.title: Specialist Referral
cards.description: When needed, we coordinate referrals to ENT specialists or neurologists for deeper care.

cards.title: Baseline Tracking
cards.description: Establish a precise audiogram baseline to track changes over time and guide future care decisions.

detailSection.title: A Full Picture of Your Hearing Health
detailSection.description: Our multi-point testing approach ensures nothing is missed — from mild high-frequency loss to complex auditory processing disorders.

cards.title: Case History & Intake
cards.description: Comprehensive review of symptoms, lifestyle, occupational noise exposure, and family hearing history.

cards.title: Pure Tone Audiometry
cards.description: Measures your hearing threshold at multiple frequencies to create a precise audiogram profile.

cards.title: Speech Audiometry
cards.description: Evaluates how well you understand speech at varying volumes in both quiet and noisy environments.

cards.title: Tympanometry
cards.description: Assesses middle ear function and eardrum mobility to detect conductive hearing issues.

cards.title: Auditory Processing
cards.description: Identifies central auditory processing disorders that standard tests may not detect.

cards.title: Results & Report
cards.description: Receive a detailed audiological report with findings, diagnosis, and personalized recommendations.

PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.title: Expert Hearing Aid Fitting & Verification
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.intro: Getting the right device is only half the journey. Precise fitting and real-ear verification ensure your hearing aids are actually performing the way your audiogram demands — not just approximating it.
list: Ear measurements and impressions when required
Careful selection and programming of hearing solutions
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.closing: This process ensures that any recommended solution is clinically appropriate and properly validated.

stats.description: Of hearing aids fitted without real-ear verification
Better speech clarity with proper REM fitting
Days adjustment period for most patients
Of our fittings include verification protocols

signsSection.heading: Prescription-Fit, Not Just Plugged In
listItems: Real-ear measurement ensures devices match your unique ear canal acoustics
Avoids over- or under-amplification that leads to dissatisfaction
Increases hearing aid acceptance and daily usage rates
Follows best practice guidelines from AAA and ASHA
Personalized programming for phone calls, music, and crowds
Includes Bluetooth and connectivity setup if applicable

cards.title: All Device Types Supported
cards.description: BTE, RIC, ITE, IIC, and CIC — our audiologists are trained across all major hearing aid styles and manufacturers.

cards.title: Smart Device Integration
cards.description: Full setup for smartphone apps, remote programming, and wireless accessories that connect to your lifestyle.

cards.title: Unlimited Fine-Tuning
cards.description: Included follow-up appointments ensure your hearing aids continue to perform as your needs evolve.

detailSection.title: From Selection to Satisfaction
detailSection.description: We follow a structured, clinically-validated protocol to match your hearing devices to your lifestyle and audiological needs.

cards.title: Needs Assessment
cards.description: We review your audiogram, lifestyle, dexterity, and communication goals to guide device selection.

cards.title: Device Selection
cards.description: Choose from a curated range of hearing aids matched to your specific hearing profile and preferences.

cards.title: Real-Ear Measurement
cards.description: Probe microphones measure actual sound levels in your ear canal to verify your prescription is met.

cards.title: Programming & Tuning
cards.description: Devices are fine-tuned to your exact audiogram with adjustments for different listening environments.

cards.title: Trial & Adjustment
cards.description: A supervised trial period lets you experience your hearing aids in real-life settings with follow-up tweaks.

cards.title: Final Verification
cards.description: Outcome measures confirm improved speech understanding before the fitting is finalized.

PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.title: Sustained Hearing Rehabilitation & Care
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.intro: Hearing better isn't a one-time event — it's an ongoing journey. Our rehabilitation programs and long-term care model ensure you continue to thrive as your hearing needs evolve.
list: Adjustments based on lifestyle and hearing changes
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.closing: This approach helps patients adapt effectively and maintain long-term hearing health.

stats.description: Average auditory adjustment period for new users
Of hearing aid users abandon devices without follow-up
Success rate with structured rehabilitation programs
Ongoing care available with your HearCog provider

signsSection.heading: Hearing Loss Is a Lifelong Condition, Not a One-Time Fix
listItems: Hearing naturally changes over time — your care should adapt with it
Auditory training significantly improves speech understanding in noise
Regular maintenance extends hearing aid lifespan and performance
Counseling reduces the anxiety and social isolation hearing loss can cause
Family education improves communication and reduces frustration
Ongoing support increases hearing aid satisfaction and usage rates

cards.title: Aural Rehabilitation Classes
cards.description: Group or individual sessions covering speechreading, listening strategies, and assistive technology use.

cards.title: Family Involvement
cards.description: We include family members in education sessions so your support network can communicate more effectively with you.

cards.title: Remote Care Options
cards.description: Telehealth check-ins and remote programming allow care to continue wherever you are without clinic visits.

detailSection.title: Your Path to Sustained Better Hearing
detailSection.description: Our comprehensive rehabilitation services address the cognitive, emotional, and practical aspects of living well with hearing loss.

cards.title: Auditory Training
cards.description: Structured listening exercises that help your brain relearn how to process sounds and speech more effectively.

cards.title: Communication Strategies
cards.description: Learn practical techniques for improving conversations in difficult listening environments, at work and at home.

cards.title: Regular Check-ins
cards.description: Scheduled follow-up appointments track your progress and adjust your hearing aids as your hearing changes.

cards.title: Counseling & Support
cards.description: Emotional support for patients and families adjusting to hearing loss, with connections to peer support groups.

cards.title: Device Maintenance
cards.description: Regular cleaning, repairs, and software updates to keep your hearing technology performing at its best.

cards.title: Annual Re-Evaluation
cards.description: Yearly audiological assessment to detect changes and update your treatment plan accordingly.

PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.title: Trusted Network of Excellence & Collaboration
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.intro: HearCog connects you with rigorously vetted hearing health care professionals who meet the highest standards of clinical excellence, ethical practice, and patient-centered care.
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.closing: By strengthening professional collaboration, we help improve care quality across the entire hearing healthcare journey.
PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.buttonText: Learn How We Support Quality Care

stats.description: Verified hearing health care professionals in our national network
Average patient satisfaction rating across providers
Of providers hold current state licensure and certifications
Patient support and care coordination available

signsSection.heading: Your Health Deserves Verified Excellence
listItems: All providers undergo credential verification and background checks
Transparent pricing and no-surprise billing practices
Evidence-based treatment recommendations, not sales quotas
Full disclosure of device options from multiple manufacturers
Patient data privacy and HIPAA compliance guaranteed
Clear care coordination with your primary care physician

cards.title: National Network
cards.description: Access to vetted hearing care professionals across the country, all meeting the same rigorous standards.

cards.title: Specialist Coordination
cards.description: Direct communication channels with ENT physicians and other medical specialists for complex cases.

cards.title: Patient Advocacy
cards.description: Your HearCog provider acts as your advocate, ensuring you receive the care you deserve.

detailSection.title: What Makes a HearCog Provider
detailSection.description: We maintain strict quality standards to ensure every patient receives exceptional, evidence-based hearing care.

cards.title: Clinical Credentials
cards.description: All providers hold current state licensure and nationally recognized certifications in audiology.

cards.title: Best Practice Compliance
cards.description: Regular audits ensure adherence to AAA, ASHA, and manufacturer best practice guidelines.

cards.title: Continuing Education
cards.description: Mandatory annual training keeps providers current on the latest hearing care technologies and methods.

cards.title: Patient Experience Reviews
cards.description: Ongoing patient feedback and satisfaction tracking to maintain quality standards across the network.

cards.title: Multidisciplinary Coordination
cards.description: Seamless collaboration with ENTs, neurologists, and other specialists for comprehensive care.

cards.title: Ethical Practice Standards
cards.description: Commitment to transparency, informed consent, and patient-centered care in all clinical decisions.
